channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: CISA orders feds to patch n8n RCE flaw exploited in attacks
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) ordered government agencies on Wednesday to patch their systems against an actively exploited n8n vulnerability. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Medtech giant Stryker offline after Iran-linked wiper malware attack
item.description: Leading medical technology company Stryker has been hit by a wiper malware attack claimed by Handala, an Iranian-linked and pro-Palestinian hacktivist group. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New PhantomRaven NPM attack wave steals dev data via 88 packages
item.description: New attack waves from the 'PhantomRaven' supply-chain campaign are hitting the npm registry, with dozens of malicious packages that exfiltrate sensitive data from JavaScript developers. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Researchers Trick Perplexity's Comet AI Browser Into Phishing Scam in Under Four Minutes
item.description: Agentic web browsers that leverage artificial intelligence (AI) capabilities to autonomously execute actions across multiple websites on behalf of a user could be trained and tricked into falling prey...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
item.description: A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports o...
item.dc:creator: Krebs on Security

item.title: Critical n8n Flaws Allow Remote Code Execution and Exposure of Stored Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of two now-patched security flaws in the n8n workflow automation platform, including two critical bugs that could result in arbitrary command execution...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Chinese Nexus Actors Shift Focus to Qatar Amid Iranian Conflict
item.description: Two attacks on Qatari entities signal a shift in focus for China-backed actors and demonstrate how quickly they can pivot in response to geopolitical events....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Meta adds new WhatsApp, Facebook, and Messenger anti-scam tools
item.description: Meta is introducing new anti-scam protections across its platforms, deploying systems and user-facing warnings to protect users against scammers. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Meta Disables 150K Accounts Linked to Southeast Asia Scam Centers in Global Crackdown
item.description: Meta on Wednesday said it disabled over 150,000 accounts associated with scam centers in Southeast Asia as part of a coordinated effort in partnership with authorities from Thailand, the U.S., the U.K...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Dozens of Vendors Patch Security Flaws Across Enterprise Software and Network Devices
item.description: SAP has released security updates to address two critical security flaws that could be exploited to achieve arbitrary code execution on affected systems. The vulnerabilities in question listed below -...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: What Boards Must Demand in the Age of AI-Automated Exploitation
item.description: “You knew, and you could have acted. Why didn’t you?” This is the question you do not want to be asked. And increasingly, it’s the question leaders are forced to answer after an incident. For years, ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft Patches 84 Flaws in March Patch Tuesday, Including Two Public Zero-Days
item.description: Microsoft on Tuesday released patches for a set of 84 new security vulnerabilities affecting various software components, including two that have been listed as publicly known. Of these, eight are rat...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: UNC6426 Exploits nx npm Supply-Chain Attack to Gain AWS Admin Access in 72 Hours
item.description: A threat actor known as UNC6426 leveraged keys stolen following the supply chain compromise of the nx npm package last year to completely breach a victim's cloud environment within a span of 72 hours....
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Five Malicious Rust Crates and AI Bot Exploit CI/CD Pipelines to Steal Developer Secrets
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered five malicious Rust crates that masquerade as time-related utilities to transmit .env file data to the threat actors. The Rust packages, published to crates.i...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Middle East Conflict Highlights Cloud Resilience Gaps
item.description: Data centers — used by both governments and militaries for operations — are now fair game, not just for cyberattacks, but for kinetic attacks as well....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Microsoft Patches 83 CVEs in March Update
item.description: For a change, there's little in this month's Patch Tuesday that should cause panic, according to security experts....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday, March 2026 Edition
item.description: Microsoft Corp. today pushed security updates to fix at least 77 vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and other software. There are no pressing "zero-day" flaws this month (compared to Feb...
item.dc:creator: Krebs on Security

item.title: New ‘BlackSanta’ EDR killer spotted targeting HR departments
item.description: For more than a year, a Russian-speaking threat actor targeted human resource (HR) departments with malware that delivers a new EDR killer named BlackSanta. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New BeatBanker Android malware poses as Starlink app to hijack devices
item.description: A new Android malware named BeatBanker can hijack devices and tricks users into installing it by posing as a Starlink app on websites masquerading as the official Google Play Store. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 'Overly Permissive' Salesforce Cloud Configs in the Crosshairs
item.description: Some customers have mishandled guest user configurations otherwise intended to allow third-party access to important — and sensitive — client data....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: New 'Zombie ZIP' technique lets malware slip past security tools
item.description: A new technique dubbed "Zombie ZIP" helps conceal payloads in compressed files specially created to avoid detection from security solutions such as antivirus and endpoint detection and response (EDR) ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Russian Threat Actor Sednit Resurfaces With Sophisticated Toolkit
item.description: After several years of using simple implants, the Russia-affiliated actor is back with two new sophisticated malware tools....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Microsoft releases Windows 10 KB5078885 extended security update
item.description: Microsoft has released the Windows 10 KB5078885 extended security update to fix the March 2026 Patch Tuesday vulnerabilities, including 2 zero-days and an issue that prevent some devices from shutting...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft March 2026 Patch Tuesday fixes 2 zero-days, 79 flaws
item.description: Today is Microsoft's March 2026 Patch Tuesday with security updates for 79 flaws, including 2 publicly disclosed zero-day vulnerabilities. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Windows 11 KB5079473 & KB5078883 cumulative updates released
item.description: Microsoft has released Windows 11 KB5079473 and KB5078883 cumulative updates for versions 25H2/24H2 and 23H2 to fix security vulnerabilities, bugs, and add new features. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: HPE warns of critical AOS-CX flaw allowing admin password resets
item.description: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) has patched multiple security vulnerabilities in the Aruba Networking AOS-CX operating system, including several authentication and code execution issues. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: FortiGate Devices Exploited to Breach Networks and Steal Service Account Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers are calling attention to a new campaign where threat actors are abusing FortiGate Next-Generation Firewall (NGFW) appliances as entry points to breach victim networks. The a...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: How to Stop AI Data Leaks: A Webinar Guide to Auditing Modern Agentic Workflows
item.description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is no longer just a tool we talk to; it is a tool that does things for us. These are called AI Agents. They can send emails, move data, and even manage software on their o...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: KadNap Malware Infects 14,000+ Edge Devices to Power Stealth Proxy Botnet
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new malware called KadNap that's primarily targeting Asus routers to enlist them into a botnet for proxying malicious traffic. The malware, first detected i...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft brings phishing-resistant Windows sign-ins via Entra passkeys
item.description: Microsoft is rolling out passkey support for Microsoft Entra on Windows devices, adding phishing-resistant passwordless authentication via Windows Hello. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
